mentions.note: Pursuing Warner Bros Discovery acquisition; competing with Paramount SkyDance on deal pricing; earnings after close Tuesday.
Fang+ original constituent; suffering P/E compression like Mag 7 peers; Cramer expects eventual reflow once storage rotation exhausts.
Robert asks if 2026 will see recovery after stock split pain. Conference call this week was show of force — Netflix completely on its game. Buyer here.
Caller asked about post-split pullback; Cramer likes Ted Sarandos strategy and Warner Bros./Zaslav content deal; pull trigger on weakness.
Leads by example on conference calls with clear narrative; balances analyst detail with retail audience needs; model for executive communication.
Original FANG member introduced Feb 2013. Cramer recommended when already established; obvious streaming leader with secular growth; proven pricing power and content moat.
Caller noted stock at $83, down 11% post-auction headlines, suggesting reversion below fair value. Cramer flagged as worthy of deeper analysis but no recommendation yet.
Down 13%—good entry point. Warner Bros. acquisition debate was prudent; core streaming fundamentals intact and undervalued at current levels.
Cramer wants to buy Netflix. Warner Bros. studio contemplation was prudent optionality review; down 13% is good entry; will outperform.

Peak screen-time concern: despite being MVP (most value for price), 100X data shows consumers planning less overall screen consumption. Reed Hastings said if tide goes out, Netflix loses—category headwind emerging.
Business isn't actually slowing; the stock is just caught in the market's broader rotation away from Mag 7/FAANG names tied to the failed Warner Bros. Discovery bid.
Walking away from WB bid good for balance sheet; stock moved 10% but Cramer would buy at current levels.
$2.8B Warner breakup fee, $20B content investment, ad revenue doubling plans support upside; leg into position slowly in weak market to avoid all-in at wrong time.
Juggernaut; came out of nowhere to build greatest entertainment company on earth; broke up with Warner Bros but collected $2.8B breakup fee; can keep doing what doing; give benefit of doubt.
Jennifer's portfolio; entertainment company; keep as part of media/entertainment diversification.
